report_metadata.title: 2026 Global Threat Report: Year of the Evasive Adversary
report_metadata.attributed_actor: Multiple actors including SCATTERED SPIDER, BLOCKADE SPIDER, PUNK SPIDER, COZY BEAR, FANCY BEAR, PRESSURE CHOLLIMA, OPERATOR PANDA, GRACEFUL SPIDER, VICE SPIDER

iocs.context: A code injection vulnerability in Langflow exploited by Cerber ransomware operators to establish persistence and deploy malware.
An unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability exploited by OPERATOR PANDA six days after PoC release.
A file upload vulnerability exploited by PHANTOM PANDA three days after public disclosure.
Known as React2Shell, an insecure deserialization vulnerability exploited by VAULT PANDA and GENESIS PANDA two days after disclosure.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA to exploit VPN appliances for initial access.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA to exploit VPN appliances for initial access.
Likely exploited by HOLLOW PANDA to compromise Check Point VPN appliances.
A critical command injection vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS exploited by OPERATOR PANDA.
A Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) RCE vulnerability exploited by VEILED PANDA.
A vulnerability in network devices exploited as a zero-day for initial access.
A vulnerability in network devices exploited as a zero-day for initial access.
A novel zero-day vulnerability in E-Business Suite (EBS) exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER for data exfiltration operations.
Vulnerability in Accellion FTA exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER.
Vulnerability in GoAnywhere MFT exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER.
Vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER.
A zero-day Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability in the Windows Common Log File System (CLFS) driver exploited by VICE SPIDER.
An n-day LPE vulnerability in the Windows CLFS driver leveraged by VICE SPIDER prior to using zero-days.
An n-day LPE vulnerability in Netlogon (Zerologon) leveraged by VICE SPIDER.

mitre_mappings.report_context: CHATTY SPIDER used voice phishing (vishing) campaigns to persuade employees to install RMM tooling.
Adversaries like CHATTY SPIDER trick users into installing legitimate remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools for initial access and control.
After an initial attempt using WinSCP was blocked, CHATTY SPIDER pivoted to using Google Drive for data exfiltration.
Multiple adversaries, including PRESSURE CHOLLIMA and FAMOUS CHOLLIMA, compromised upstream providers, development ecosystems, and public code repositories (like npm) to distribute malicious code.
eCrime and nation-state actors (COZY BEAR, IMPERIAL KITTEN) leveraged AiTM phishing kits to intercept authentication processes, capturing session tokens and credentials in real time.
COZY BEAR used phishing links based on OAuth 2.0 authorization and device code flows to steal tokens and gain access to Microsoft 365.
SCATTERED SPIDER mounted a domain controller's virtual disk (VMDK) to an unmanaged VM to directly copy the ntds.dit Active Directory database.
Multiple BGH adversaries, including PUNK SPIDER and BLOCKADE SPIDER, deployed ransomware (Akira, Embargo) to encrypt data on victim systems, often targeting ESXi servers and remote SMB shares.
PUNK SPIDER executed Akira ransomware from an unmanaged host to remotely encrypt files on managed hosts via SMB shares, avoiding detection on the target endpoints.
BLOCKADE SPIDER modified Entra ID conditional access policies to weaken security controls.
HOLLOW PANDA deployed ShadowPad malware by using DLL search-order hijacking after gaining initial access to Check Point VPN appliances.
HOLLOW PANDA configured its ShadowPad implant to use DNS tunneling for command and control communications.
BLOCKADE SPIDER created mail forwarding and deletion rules in Microsoft 365 to exfiltrate data and hide their activity from legitimate users.
SCATTERED SPIDER used social engineering to persuade help desk personnel to perform self-service password resets on target accounts, bypassing MFA.

paths.description: A social engineering-led attack abusing help desk and cloud identities to access and steal AD credentials from an unmanaged VM, leading to ransomware deployment.
A state-sponsored espionage campaign focused on rapidly exploiting edge device vulnerabilities for persistent access and C2.
A sophisticated phishing campaign by COZY BEAR using AiTM and social engineering to steal OAuth tokens for cloud access.
An eCrime TTP where adversaries like VICE SPIDER exploit a local vulnerability to escalate privileges on a compromised Windows host.
